achieve this feat5. Hence, in 2015, she was awarded the Padma Shri, the fourth highest civilian award in India. She was also awarded the Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award and National Award for Best Female Mountaineer. 	5. feat: achievementThe India BookmarkThe Himalayas are among the world's youngest mountain ranges and are still growing today. Scientists estimate that some parts rise by a few millimetres each year due to the movement of tectonic plates.Arunima hails from Ambedkar Nagar, a small district in Uttar Pradesh. Her father was an army engineer, and her mother worked as a health supervisor. Sports were a significant part of Arunima’s life, with her family having a natural inclination towards athletics. After completing her law degree following her post-graduation, Arunima decided to pursue a job in the paramilitary forces, which would allow her to continue her passion for sports while having a regular income. On the fateful night of 11 April 2011, she met with a train accident. The hours that followed were harrowing6. She never imagined she would survive that night, but survive she did, miraculously, displaying an extraordinary will to live. Arunima received a prosthetic leg and underwent physiotherapy. It was during this period that she made a profound decision. Inspired by an article about Mount Everest, she resolved to conquer the highest peak in the world. To pursue her ambition, Arunima Sinha completed a basic mountaineering course at the Nehru Institute of Mountaineering in Uttarkashi, followed by eighteen months of rigorous7 training. Arunima approached 6. harrowing: extremely Bachendri Pal, the first Indian woman to summit Everest, to guide disturbing and painful her in the endeavour8. Bachendri Pal instilled confidence in Arunima 7. rigorous: severe and said, “My child, you decided to climb Mount Everest in these situations with a prosthetic leg—you have conquered the Everest within ? strength in achieving difficult goals?Why is mental strength as important as physical you; the world will realise it later.” On 21 May 2013, after 52 exhausting days, Arunima Sinha reached the summit of Mount Everest. Standing at the top of the world, she felt a surge9 of emotions—pride, relief, and an overwhelming sense of achievement. She displayed the Indian flag at the summit and left behind a photograph of her idol, Swami Vivekananda, whose teachings had inspired her throughout her 8. endeavour: an attempt to do something journey.Arunima’s feat did not end with Everest. By July 2016, she had summited seven peaks in Europe, Africa, Australia, South America, North America, and of course, Mount Everest in Asia. In January 2019, she climbed the seventh peak on Antarctica and became world’s first female amputee to climb Mount Vinson. Her goal to complete the Seven Summits was achieved.Throughout her journey, Arunima has faced numerous challenges and setbacks. Yet, her story is not just about physical endurance10 surge: a sudden and great increaseendurance: (here) ability to continue doing something difficult for a long timebut also about mental fortitude11 and the power of a positive mind set. Her message is simple yet profound: “Never give up and work hard. Always remember your goal and work on it.” 	11. fortitude: courage over a long periodArunima’s autobiography, Born Again on the Mountain: A Story of Losing Everything and Finding It Back records her incredible journey to the top of the world. It is a story of triumph over adversity12, a testament13 to the human spirit’s capacity to rise above the most challenging circumstances. 	12. adversity: a difficult situationShe was conferred14 with the honorary doctorate by the University of Strathclyde, Glasgow, UK. In her words, “Failure is not when we fall short of achieving our goals. It is when we don’t have goals worthy enough. Never forget your goal, respect it, 13. testament: proof work hard, and you will be the winner.” Dr. Arunima Sinha has not 14. conferred: given an only conquered mountains but has also won people’s hearts the official title or honour world over, inspiring them to reach their own summits.Learning Ask a sports coach or physical trainer: What kind of physical and Through mental training is needed to prepare for a difficult challenge like mountain Interaction	climbing?Arunima Sinha meets with a terrible accident and loses a legHer journey becomes an inspiring example of courage, determination, and hope.While recovering in hospital, she decides to climb Mount EverestAt last, she reaches the top of Mount Everest and creates historyShe gets a prosthetic leg and begins mountaineering trainingHer strong will keeps her moving towards the summitWith courage and hard work, she prepares herself for the great challengeShe faces pain, difficulty and harsh conditions during the climbStory JourneyRead the question carefully and darken the  circle of the correct answer in the OMR sheet. What was Arunima Sinha’s profession before the accident?TeacherDoctorNational-level volleyball playerMountaineerAt what age did Arunima face the life-altering accident?20 	(b) 22 	(c) 24 	(d) 26In which year did Arunima climb Mount Everest?2011 	(b) 2012 	(c) 2013 	(d) 2015Who guided Arunima in her mountaineering journey?Tenzing Norgay (b) Bachendri Pal 	(c) Edmund Hillary 	(d) Major SinghHow many days did it take her to reach the summit?30 days 	(b) 40 days 	(c) 60 days 	(d) 52 daysWrite ‘T’ for true and ‘F’ for false statements. CG:2, C:2.2She lost one of her legs after being pushed from a moving train. Arunima trained only for a few weeks before attempting Everest. Arunima is the first Indian female amputee to climb Mount Everest. Arunima received the Padma Shri award in 2015. Arunima decided to give up sports after the accident. Find lines from the story that tell you: Arunima survived a terrible accident through sheer willpower.Arunima’s achievement became a source of inspiration for others.Reaching the summit was an emotional moment for Arunima.Arunima’s success was not only physical but mental too.Arunima inspires people everywhere.Answer the following questions  What did Arunima do in order to fulfill her ambition of conquering Mount Everest? Arunima is a beacon of hope and resilience. Support this statement with evidence from the text. Arunima’s autobiography is titled Born Again on the Mountain: A Story of Losing Everything and Finding It Back. Why do you think this title is appropriate?  Arunima says, “Failure is when we don’t have goals worthy enough.” What would be the most likely impact of this statement on readers?Describe the training process Arunima underwent to prepare for her goal.Why was climbing Mount Everest considered especially difficult for Arunima? What difficulties did she face during the Everest expedition?	Put on Your Thinking Caps	 	/Complete the ‘My Summit Plan’ given below by thinking about a goal you want to achieve. My goal: ______________________________Biggest obstacle: ________________________  	• Skills I need: ___________________________Steps I will take:______________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________	Dictionary & Vocabulary Skills	  	/Use a dictionary to find the noun and adjective forms of the words given below. Then complete the table. If more than one form is listed in the dictionary, choose the one that best matches the meaning used in the chapter.Base WordRelated NounRelated Adjectivebrave succeeddetermineachieveEach sentence has one spelling error. Correct it and explain how it affects meaning. 	1. The climbers showed great determenation during the ascent.   ___________________ 	2. The mountain peak looked magnifisent at sunrise. ___________________ 	3. They carried neccessary equipment for survival. ___________________ 	4. The journy to the summit was exhausting. ___________________ 	5. The climbers moved cautiosly on the narrow ridge. ___________________ 	6. The icy winds were unbareble at high altitude. ___________________	Grammar Lab	  	/Complete the following table by filling in the gaps with correct parts of speech mentioned. An example has been done for you.NounsAdjectivesVerbs (present)AdverbsdeterminationdetermineddeterminedeterminedlyreliefrelievedlyinspiringinspireresistanceresistantlysuccesssuccessfulScan & LearnScan the QR code to access Ask questions, clear yout the worksheet for this doubts, and learn more with chapter.AI.
